title: Kung Pao Chicken Zoodles For Two
description: This Chinese-American favorite gets a healthy makeover with spiralized zucchini noodles replacing traditional wheat noodles. Tender chicken pieces are stir-fried with crisp bell peppers in a savory-sweet kung pao sauce, then tossed with fresh zoodles for a satisfying meal under 300 calories per serving.
ingredients: 1/2 tbsp Sambal Oelek red chili paste
2 medium zucchini (about 8 oz each, ends trimmed)
6 oz skinless chicken breasts (cut into 1/2-inch pieces)
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper (to taste)
2 tbsp crushed dry roasted peanuts (for topping)
2 tbsp thinly sliced scallions along diagonal (for topping)
instructions: Prep the zucchini noodles using a spiralizer with the thickest noodle blade, or a mandolin fitted with a julienne blade, cutting 2 medium zucchini into long spaghetti-like strips. Cut the strands into pieces about 6 to 8 inches long with kitchen scissors for easier eating. Set aside on a clean towel or paper towels and pat dry to prevent a watery final dish.

Make the sauce by whisking together 1 1/2 tbsp reduced-sodium soy sauce, 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar, 1 tsp hoisin sauce, 2 1/2 tbsp water, 1/2 tbsp Sambal Oelek, 2 tsp sugar, and 2 tsp cornstarch in a small bowl until smooth with no cornstarch lumps. Set aside.

Cook the chicken by seasoning 6 oz chicken pieces with salt and pepper to taste. Heat 1 tsp grapeseed or canola oil in a large, deep nonstick pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add the chicken in a single layer without crowding and cook until browned and cooked through to internal temperature of 165°F, about 4 to 5 minutes. Transfer to a plate and set aside.

Build the stir-fry by reducing heat to medium. Add 1 tsp sesame oil, 2 cloves minced garlic, and 1 tsp grated ginger to the skillet and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds, stirring constantly to avoid burning. Add 1/2 red bell pepper pieces, then re-whisk the sauce and pour it in. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until thickened and bubbling, about 1-2 minutes.

Add zoodles and finish by stirring in the zucchini noodles and cooking while tossing frequently for about 2 minutes until just tender and coated with sauce. Do not overcook as the zucchini will release moisture. Return the cooked chicken to the pan and toss to combine. Divide between 2 bowls and top with 2 tbsp crushed peanuts and 2 tbsp sliced scallions.
notes: For gluten-free: use tamari instead of soy sauce and ensure hoisin sauce is gluten-free
Re-whisk the sauce right before adding to the pan as cornstarch settles quickly
Don't overcook the zoodles - two minutes of tossing is plenty to avoid mushiness
For peanut allergy substitute toasted cashews or omit entirely
Start with 1/2 tbsp Sambal Oelek and add more after tasting if you want more heat
